Jae Woong Hwang

Hardware-Aware Software Engineer | Vehicle Controls & AI Toolchains

Hardware-aware software engineer building vehicle control systems and AI-ready validation toolchains.

I connect real vehicle hardware, embedded control software, model-based development, and Python/C++ automation to remove R&D bottlenecks.

Hardware-Software Integration Vehicle Dynamics Embedded Control Model-Based Development ADAS Lvl2 MILS/HILS XCP / CAN-FD Python / C++ MATLAB / Simulink EKF ONNX MF4 A2L / ELF

Core Positioning

  • Hardware-aware software engineer who understands physical vehicle constraints, not only office-side code.
  • Builder of internal tools that remove repetitive development, validation, and calibration bottlenecks.
  • Systems architect who uses Claude/Codex effectively because the target architecture and constraints are clear.
  • Fast learner who converts new technologies into practical engineering workflows.

Education

University of California Los Angeles (UCLA) B.S. Mechanical Engineering | 2010.09 - 2014.06
Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST) M.S. Robotics | 2015.03 - 2017.08 Thesis: Controller Design for Motion Stabilization of a Turret on a Moving Platform

Skill Stack

Control: Vehicle dynamics · Transmission control · SCC/LFA ADAS logic · Sensor fusion · EKF · Time Delay Estimation

Validation: MILS · HILS · Dynamometer · Vehicle test · Calibration · Mass-production sign-off

Software: Python · C/C++ · MATLAB/Simulink · XCP/CAN-FD · ONNX · A2L/ELF · MF4

Experience

Researcher - KAIST Master's Research Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST) | 2015.03 - 2017.08
Detail

Embedded motion-control foundation: designed a controller for real turret hardware under moving-platform disturbance.

  • Designed controller for motion stabilization of a turret on a moving platform.
  • Worked across dynamics, control logic, sensor noise, and real hardware validation.
  • Built the control foundation later reused in vehicle dynamics and sensor-fusion work.

Detail: experience/kaist-masters/index.html

Researcher - TCU Application Software Agency for Defense Development (ADD), Ground Technology Research Institute | 2017.09 - 2021.03
Detail

Full V-cycle TCU productization: developed K2 55-ton tracked-vehicle TCU software from MILS/HILS to dynamometer and field validation.

  • Developed TCU application software for K2 tracked vehicle transmission control.
  • Experienced full V-cycle workflow: MILS, HILS, powerpack dynamometer, and actual vehicle field testing.
  • Worked on control algorithms, plant modeling, calibration, parameter correction, and reliability-test support.

Detail: experience/add-k2-tcu/index.html

Research Engineer - Commercial Vehicle SCC / Lvl2 ADAS Hyundai Motor Company (HMC) | 2021.03 - Present
Detail

Production commercial-vehicle ADAS engineer with SCC part experience and independent ownership of Emergency Stop, EKF RoadRadius fusion, FOC brake-distribution logic, and target-selection robustness.

  • Worked in the SCC part of HMC's commercial-vehicle SCC/LFA development organization, contributing to mass-production SCC/FOC tuning, regulation-driven state-transition updates, validation, and production issue response.
  • Contributed to mass-production programs across QZ FCEV Europe, QZ FCEV North America, QZ 25PE ICE tractor, and PY ICE/FCEV bus platforms with different AT/AMT and EBS/ABS configurations.
  • Independently developed SCC2 Emergency Stop behavior, including activation, state-transition handling, controlled deceleration, and safe-stop flow.
  • Developed EKF-based RoadRadius fusion combining steering-intent-based radius estimation, yaw-rate-based radius estimation, and conditional lane/camera blending.
  • Developed rule-based FOC brake distribution logic balancing auxiliary brake and foundation brake usage under heavy-duty deceleration scenarios.
  • Built target object selection robustness logic to reduce adjacent-lane false target risk in difficult commercial-vehicle road scenarios.

Detail: experience/hmc-adas/index.html

Engineering Narrative

My career is a hardware-to-software arc: from real motion-control hardware, to full V-cycle vehicle TCU software, to commercial-vehicle ADAS and AI-ready validation platforms.

  • Physical constraints -> control logic: I turn hardware limitations, sensing delays, and vehicle dynamics into algorithms and validation evidence.
  • R&D bottlenecks -> toolchains: I build internal Python/C++/MATLAB platforms when repeated manual work slows the team.
  • System architecture first: I use AI coding assistants effectively because I define the full data path, interfaces, timing constraints, and validation goal before coding.
  • NDA-safe technical storytelling: I explain protected work through problem definition, architecture, math/logic abstraction, and measurable outcome.

Selected Toolchain Projects

Main-root vehicle projects are covered in Experience. This section highlights independently built toolchains that removed team bottlenecks.

E2E GUI XCP Bypassing App Python / C++ / XCP / CAN-FD / ONNX
Detail MOCK

Laptop-only real-vehicle AI bypass testing on mass-production ADAS ECU.

Objective: Commercial-vehicle ADAS needed an E2E transition path without relying on scarce passenger-car AI hardware such as Orin/Thor setups. I designed a platform where engineers can connect a laptop through Vector/Kvaser XCP to a mass-production ADAS ECU and test learned models on real vehicles.

Impact: Reduces dependency on special devices and CANape workflows, enables AI model testing across many vehicle variants with only a laptop and ECU connection, and prepares the team to move from rule-based logic toward E2E AI validation.

Python C++ XCP CAN-FD ONNX A2L/ELF

Detail: toolchains/e2e-xcp-bypass/index.html

Mock: _toolchain_ui_mock/01_xcp_ai_validation_ui/index.html

E2E GUI Time-Series AI Model Training Platform Python / MF4 / PyTorch / ONNX / MATLAB Export
Detail MOCK

All-in-one MF4 time-series preprocessing, training, normalization, and deployment conversion platform.

Objective: The team works mostly with CANape MF4 time-series data. I built a customizable platform that lets engineers load, preprocess, normalize, train, and export models without rewriting the same data pipeline for each ADAS module.

Impact: Gives each module owner a reusable path to test AI-based replacements for rule-based logic, then export .pt to ONNX or MATLAB-compatible formats for simulation and real-vehicle bypass testing.

Python MF4 PyTorch ONNX Time-Series

Detail: toolchains/timeseries-ai-training/index.html

Mock: _toolchain_ui_mock/02_timeseries_training_ui/index.html

TOS/ODP MATLAB Simulink Bird-Eye-View Simulator MATLAB Script / Simulink / ADAS Visualization
Detail MOCK

Repeatable simulator for rare bus-only-lane misdetection scenarios that were hard to reproduce with real vehicles.

Objective: During PY bus development, repeated bus-only-lane misdetections required a way to visualize tracks, cut-in/cut-out thresholds, and ODP/TOS behavior without repeatedly deploying a massive bus into rare traffic conditions.

Impact: Enabled repeated logic modification and parameter tuning in simulation before final vehicle confirmation, helping the team obtain test-team sign-off for mass-production bus behavior.

MATLAB Simulink ADAS BEV TOS/ODP

Detail: toolchains/tos-odp-bev-simulator/index.html
